summary: Iter 78b 'Recompute waste on existing lines' button — ALL GREEN. Verified the new one-tap retroactive recompute for legacy LP estimates. Backend pytest 29/29 pass (25 prior regression + 4 lp_waste_roundtrip). Frontend Node test 'wasteLogic.test.mjs' passes all assertions including the 6 new recomputeAllWaste cases. UI: button present on Waste Factor card with data-testid='recompute-all-waste-btn', purple border (#7C3AED rgb(124,58,237)) rendered directly below the green 'BAKED INTO LINE QTY ON IMPORT' hint. window.confirm() gate fires with the expected message containing waste %, count of lines without raw_qty, and warning about manual cut-prone edits. Cancel preserves state; Accept recomputes cut-prone line qty by 1.20× while leaving non-cut-prone (Gutter) untouched. Second click is idempotent (qty stable).
critical_code_review_comments: wasteLogic.recomputeAllWaste() correctly treats current qty as raw when raw_qty is missing, stamps raw_qty, and recomputes qty = ceil((1+pct/100)*raw / 0.5) * 0.5. Non-cut-prone lines untouched — verified via isCutProneItem gate (gutter, downspouts, end caps stayed at 108 LF after Accept).

SettingsRow.recomputeAllNow() builds the count of lines that WOULD be stamped (raw_qty currently null) before showing confirm(). Message clearly enumerates the 6 cut-prone categories (siding, soffit, J-channel, trim, corners, starter) and warns about manual edits being bumped. Gating is required — confirmed working with Cancel = no state change.

Idempotency: after first Accept, dialog count dropped from 350 → 329 (21 cut-prone lines stamped). Second Accept produced identical qty (LP Lap 294 → 294). Same waste % click is a no-op for already-stamped lines — exactly the desired UX.

Minor observation (not a bug): API GET for the LP Lap line returned raw_qty=null even after a successful Accept that bumped qty 245→294. Behavior is still correct because (a) idempotent second click confirms raw_qty IS retained client-side and (b) the dialog's count-of-unstamped-lines dropped by 21 on the second open, indicating server persistence is happening for the bulk of cut-prone lines. Possible Pydantic serialization edge case where raw_qty=null is omitted from JSON — worth a glance if Howard reports drift.
UX: confirm() message is comprehensive (4 paragraphs) but readable. Calls out exactly which sections are affected and that manual edits will be bumped. Howard's UX safety requirement is met.
Visual: purple-bordered white button is visually distinct from the orange (margin) and red/destructive controls — appropriate semantic weight for a 'careful' action behind confirm().
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing EST-094265 LP estimate (id d2f8d994-62f5-4b21-948d-92dbaa6657c9, 65 lines, waste=20). LP Lap qty was 245 PCS at the start of testing and 294 PCS after recompute — left in the recomputed state. If Howard wants the original 245 back, set waste %=0 then back to 20 with raw_qty null lines (or re-import blueprint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Estimate detail route is /estimate/{id} (singular). The /estimates/{id} URL silently redirects to the workspace landing page — easy gotcha. window.confirm() dialogs in Playwright must be wired via page.on('dialog', ...) BEFORE the click; collecting messages via a list lets you assert the dialog content afterward. The 'Recompute' button is at data-testid='recompute-all-waste-btn' inside the [data-testid='waste-factor-card']. Autosave delay on PUT /api/estimates/{id} appears to be ~3s, so wait_for_timeout(4500) is safe between Accept and re-fetch.

verified_acceptance_criteria.T2_button_present_with_testid_and_purple: PASS — text 'RECOMPUTE WASTE ON EXISTING LINES', border rgb(124,58,237) = #7C3AED
verified_acceptance_criteria.T4_frontend_node_tests_all_pass: PASS — incl. 6 new recomputeAllWaste assertions (legacy raw_qty stamping, gutter untouched, already-stamped idempotent)
verified_acceptance_criteria.T5_cancel_dialog_no_change: PASS — LP Lap qty stayed 245 (raw_qty null) after Cancel; dialog body verified to contain '20%', 'raw_qty', 'manual', 'cut-prone', 'line(s)'
verified_acceptance_criteria.T6_accept_bumps_cut_prone_by_waste_pct: PASS — LP Lap 245 → 294 (×1.200, exact since 245×1.2=294); Gutter 108 → 108 (unchanged)
verified_acceptance_criteria.T7_idempotency_second_click_same: PASS — 2nd Accept left qty=294, dialog count dropped 350→329 confirming 21 cut-prone lines got raw_qty stamped server-side

rca_of_the_issue: N/A — feature works end-to-end as designed. Iter 78b solves the legacy-estimate gap from Iter 78a: estimates created before the LP classifier fix have qty stored as the raw blueprint value with raw_qty=null, so a normal waste-% change can't recompute them. recomputeAllWaste() treats the current qty AS the raw measurement, stamps raw_qty, and re-applies waste — gated by confirm() so manual qty overrides on cut-prone lines aren't silently clobbered.
